## Test Data EDA

*Auto-generated by [analysis/MentalRiskES_test/eda_test_data.py](eda_test_data.py); re-run that script after any data changes.*

### Task 1 test corpus

- **Sessions in test data:** 10 (S01, S03, S04, S05, S06, S07, S09, S12, S15, S16)
- **Sessions in `gold_label.json`:** 17
- **Patients in gold but NOT in released test data:** S02, S08, S10, S11, S13, S14, S17 (presumably scored against zero defaults by the leaderboard — this is the ~±0.10 gap between local R30 item-MAE and the official numbers).
- **Rounds per session:** mean 56.8, min 30, max 82.
- **Patient words per session:** mean 2052, median 2161.

#### Per-session statistics

| Session | Rounds | Patient words | PHQ-9 total / band | GAD-7 total / band | CompACT-10 total |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| S01 | 63 | 2022 | 16 / moderately_severe | 16 / severe | 37 |
| S03 | 50 | 1453 | 22 / severe | 18 / severe | 38 |
| S04 | 61 | 2168 | 13 / moderate | 15 / severe | 44 |
| S05 | 48 | 2593 | 15 / moderately_severe | 16 / severe | 40 |
| S06 | 58 | 2677 | 16 / moderately_severe | 10 / moderate | 35 |
| S07 | 30 | 986 | 2 / minimal | 7 / mild | 22 |
| S09 | 67 | 2183 | 21 / severe | 19 / severe | 45 |
| S12 | 82 | 2276 | 11 / moderate | 17 / severe | 50 |
| S15 | 59 | 2005 | 11 / moderate | 16 / severe | 36 |
| S16 | 50 | 2154 | 7 / mild | 14 / moderate | 33 |

#### Severity-band distribution across the 10 test sessions

| Instrument | Band | Sessions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| PHQ-9 | minimal | 1 |
| PHQ-9 | mild | 1 |
| PHQ-9 | moderate | 3 |
| PHQ-9 | moderately_severe | 3 |
| PHQ-9 | severe | 2 |
| GAD-7 | minimal | 0 |
| GAD-7 | mild | 1 |
| GAD-7 | moderate | 2 |
| GAD-7 | severe | 7 |

**Cohort skew:** the test cohort is heavily weighted to moderate-to-severe presentations — 8 of 10 sessions land in the GAD-7 severe band (gold total ≥ 15). This is meaningfully different from the trial cohort (single moderate patient) and the simulated personas (target totals span the full range). Under-prediction by anchored systems is the dominant failure mode on this cohort, as documented in [analysis/MentalRiskES_test/SUMMARY.md §2.1](../analysis/MentalRiskES_test/SUMMARY.md).

### Task 2 test corpus

- **Patient-round pairs:** 568 across 10 sessions (rounds 1–82).
- **Labelled rounds:** 568 (every patient turn in the test data has an associated `correct_option` in `round_X_gold.json`).
- **Mean patient words per round:** 36.1.
- **Mean option length:** opt1 43.1, opt2 42.6, opt3 42.5 words.

#### Per-session statistics

| Session | Rounds | gold=1 | gold=2 | gold=3 | Mean patient words | Mean option words |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| S01 | 63 | 30 (48%) | 20 (32%) | 13 (21%) | 32.1 | 42.0 |
| S03 | 50 | 15 (30%) | 16 (32%) | 19 (38%) | 29.1 | 39.7 |
| S04 | 61 | 28 (46%) | 12 (20%) | 21 (34%) | 35.5 | 41.6 |
| S05 | 48 | 18 (38%) | 14 (29%) | 16 (33%) | 54.0 | 55.0 |
| S06 | 58 | 19 (33%) | 24 (41%) | 15 (26%) | 46.2 | 36.0 |
| S07 | 30 | 9 (30%) | 9 (30%) | 12 (40%) | 32.9 | 51.7 |
| S09 | 67 | 22 (33%) | 24 (36%) | 21 (31%) | 32.6 | 33.6 |
| S12 | 82 | 27 (33%) | 30 (37%) | 25 (30%) | 27.8 | 49.1 |
| S15 | 59 | 14 (24%) | 21 (36%) | 24 (41%) | 34.0 | 43.0 |
| S16 | 50 | 21 (42%) | 16 (32%) | 13 (26%) | 43.1 | 40.3 |
| **Total** | | **203 (35.7%)** | **186 (32.7%)** | **179 (31.5%)** | | |

#### Gold class by round tercile

| Tercile | gold=1 | gold=2 | gold=3 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| early | 89 | 85 | 96 |
| mid | 90 | 80 | 62 |
| late | 24 | 21 | 21 |

**Gold distribution is roughly uniform across classes** (35.7 / 32.8 / 31.5 % overall), in contrast to the trial corpus where option 3 is 50 %. The consensus-failure analysis in [SUMMARY.md §5.8](../analysis/MentalRiskES_test/SUMMARY.md) shows that despite this near-uniform distribution, gold=3 rounds are wrong-by-every-system 38.5 % of the time — a categorical blind spot across the LLM family rather than a class-imbalance artefact.
